event_text: Norse explorers establish a short-lived settlement at L’Anse aux Meadows in Newfoundland.
John Cabot reaches the North Atlantic coast under English commission.
Jacques Cartier explores the Gulf of St Lawrence and claims territory for France.
Port-Royal is established in Acadia.
Samuel de Champlain founds Quebec City.
Henry Hudson explores Hudson Bay.
Ville-Marie, later Montreal, is founded.
The Hudson’s Bay Company is chartered.
The Great Peace of Montreal is agreed between New France and many Indigenous nations.
The Treaty of Utrecht transfers Acadia, Newfoundland and Hudson Bay territory to Britain.
The deportation of the Acadians begins.
British forces defeat the French at the Battle of the Plains of Abraham.
The Treaty of Paris gives Britain control of New France.
The Quebec Act recognises French civil law and Catholic rights in Quebec.
Loyalist migration to British North America increases after the American Revolution.
The Constitutional Act divides Quebec into Upper Canada and Lower Canada.
The United States declares war on Britain, beginning the War of 1812 in British North America.
The 49th parallel is established as part of the US-British North American boundary.
The Lower Canada Rebellion begins, followed by further unrest in Upper Canada.
The Province of Canada is formed by uniting Upper and Lower Canada.
Responsible government is firmly established in the Province of Canada.
The Colony of British Columbia is created during the Fraser Canyon Gold Rush.
The Charlottetown and Quebec Conferences develop plans for Confederation.
Canadian Confederation creates the Dominion of Canada.
The Red River Resistance begins under Louis Riel.
Manitoba joins Confederation.
British Columbia joins Confederation.
Prince Edward Island joins Confederation.
The Indian Act is passed.
The North-West Resistance is defeated and Louis Riel is executed.
The Canadian Pacific Railway is completed.
The Klondike Gold Rush begins.
Alberta and Saskatchewan become provinces.
Canada enters the First World War when Britain declares war on Germany.
Canadian forces capture Vimy Ridge.
Women gain federal voting rights in Canada with some exclusions.
The Winnipeg General Strike becomes a major labour conflict.
Agnes Macphail becomes the first woman elected to the House of Commons.
The Chinese Immigration Act severely restricts Chinese immigration.
The Balfour Declaration recognises dominion autonomy within the British Empire.
The Persons Case recognises women as persons eligible for the Senate.
The Statute of Westminster confirms Canadian legislative independence.
Canada declares war on Germany, entering the Second World War.
Japanese Canadians are interned during the Second World War.
Canadian troops land on Juno Beach during D-Day.
Canadian citizenship is legally created.
Newfoundland joins Canada.
Lester B. Pearson helps develop United Nations peacekeeping during the Suez Crisis.
The Canadian Bill of Rights is enacted.
The maple leaf flag becomes Canada’s national flag.
Canada celebrates its centennial and Expo 67 opens in Montreal.
The Official Languages Act makes English and French official languages federally.
The October Crisis leads to the use of the War Measures Act.
Montreal hosts the Olympic Games.
Quebec votes No in the first sovereignty referendum.
Canada patriates its constitution and adopts the Charter of Rights and Freedoms.
Canada and the United States sign a free trade agreement.
The Oka Crisis highlights Indigenous land rights and tensions.
The Charlottetown Accord is rejected by referendum.
Quebec narrowly votes No in the second sovereignty referendum.
Nunavut becomes a Canadian territory.
Canada joins international military operations after the September 11 attacks.
Same-sex marriage becomes legal across Canada.
The Truth and Reconciliation Commission of Canada is established.
Vancouver hosts the Winter Olympic Games.
The Truth and Reconciliation Commission releases its final report.
Canada legalises recreational cannabis.
The COVID-19 pandemic causes major public health and economic disruption in Canada.
Tk’emlúps te Secwépemc announces findings of possible unmarked graves at Kamloops Indian Residential School.
